incident_summary: Remediation service experienced complete failure of automated remediation actions for approximately 2.5 hours due to Microsoft Graph API outage, with SEV-2 severity

root_cause.summary: Microsoft Graph API outage caused all remediation actions requiring Microsoft Graph integration to fail with 503 errors
root_cause.trigger: Microsoft Graph API service degradation (graph.microsoft.com returning HTTP 503)
root_cause.mechanism: Remediation service depends on Microsoft Graph API for executing remediation actions; when Graph API became unavailable, all dependent remediation actions failed and queued, causing action latency to spike from 1.5s baseline to 48-62s and error rate to exceed 80%

customer_impact.description: Customers experienced complete failure of automated remediation actions for detected threats. While threat detection continued normally, any automated responses requiring Microsoft Graph integration (such as user account actions, mailbox operations, or Microsoft 365 security controls) could not execute and were queued for later processing
affected_functionality: Automated remediation actions requiring Microsoft Graph API
unaffected_functionality: Threat detection and analysis (IES email security pipeline)

resolution.action_taken: No internal action required — incident resolved when Microsoft restored Graph API service availability
resolution.confirmed_by: CloudWatch logs showing HTTP 200 responses from graph.microsoft.com at 18:55 UTC; remediation action latency returned to 1.7s baseline by 19:00 UTC; backlog processing confirmed in logs at 18:58 UTC

root_cause: CloudWatch logs showing consistent HTTP 503 errors from graph.microsoft.com starting 16:25 UTC
Slack thread confirmation by engineer at 16:35 UTC and 16:40 UTC referencing Microsoft status page
PagerDuty resolution message stating 'Microsoft Graph API restored'
timeline: Detection: PagerDuty alert trigger at 16:30:00Z, Prometheus anomaly start at 16:30:00Z
Mitigation: CloudWatch INFO message at 18:55:00Z, Slack confirmation at 18:55
Recovery: Prometheus latency returned to baseline at 19:00:00Z, PagerDuty resolved at 19:00:00Z
customer_impact: Slack thread at 16:38 confirming 'detection still working fine, IES email security is unaffected. Only remediation actions are delayed'
Prometheus metrics showing ies_detection_throughput stable throughout incident
PagerDuty alert showing >80% error rate for remediation actions

data_gaps: Exact number of failed/queued remediation actions during the outage
Specific types of remediation actions affected (user actions, mailbox operations, etc.)
Whether any manual workarounds were communicated to customers during the outage
Time to fully clear the backlog after recovery (logs show draining at 18:58 but completion time unknown)
